text: The patient is positioned in the prone jackknife position, with the buttocks separated using tapes. The surgeon probes the anovaginal fistula to assess the extent of the defect.

The surgeon makes a curvilinear incision over the perineal body to isolate the anterior external sphincter. Low voltage cautery is used to raise dermal and anodermal flaps, exposing the internal sphincter. The anterior muscle disruption and scar tissue are visually identified.

The surgeon performs partial mobilization of the skin flaps in the lateral aspects, working within the subcutaneous tissue. The disrupted muscle is identified and retracted, facilitating further mobilization while ensuring the muscle is not skeletonized laterally.

The surgeon identifies the connection point between the levator muscles and the sphincter complex. During the anterior dissection, the residual internal sphincter muscle remains integrated with the anoderm, without isolation or separation.

The surgeon further mobilizes the destructed external sphincter muscle, facilitating access to the levator muscles. An identical dissection is performed on the contralateral side, demonstrating the mobilization of the external sphincter muscle.
The retracting tapes are released, allowing for a full overlap of the muscle to be achieved, enhancing the surgical field exposure.
The surgeon closes the vaginal opening using interrupted 3-0 microsutures, carefully placing each suture to ensure proper closure and alignment of the tissue edges.
The surgeon performs levatorplasty as part of the sphincter repair, using OPDS interrupted sutures. Three or four sutures are placed, ensuring the levators are not confused with the vaginal wall.
The surgeon approximates the levator muscles and performs a formal overlapping sphincteroplasty using four O-Vicro sutures, visibly securing the sphincter complex.
After completing the overlapping sphincteroplasty, the surgeon approximates the skin, focusing on the anterior anoderm defect. The wound is left partially open to allow for free drainage.
